title.en: ORGANIC FERTILISERS NOT COVERED BY SUBCLASSES C05B, C05C, e.g. FERTILISERS FROM WASTE OR REFUSE
Fertilisers made from animal corpses, or parts thereof
{from fish or from fish-wastes}
{from meat-wastes or from other wastes of animal origin, e.g. skins, hair, hoofs, feathers, blood}
{from derived products of animal origin or their wastes, e.g. leather, dairy products}
Apparatus for the manufacture
Fertilisers from human or animal excrements, e.g. manure
Guano
from human faecal masses
Apparatus for the manufacture

Fertilisers from distillery wastes, molasses, vinasses, sugar plant or similar wastes or residues, {e.g. from waste originating from industrial processing of raw material of agricultural origin or derived products thereof}
{Solid waste from mechanical processing of material, e.g. seed coats, olive pits, almond shells, fruit residue, rice hulls}
{Liquid waste from mechanical processing of material, e.g. wash-water, milling fluid, filtrate}
{Waste from chemical processing of material, e.g. diestillation, roasting, cooking}
Fertilisers from waste water, sewage sludge, sea slime, ooze or similar masses
{Waste water from industrial processing material neither of agricultural nor of animal origin}
from sulfite liquor or other waste lyes from the manufacture of cellulose
from waste liquors in the potash industry
Fertilisers from household or town refuse
Apparatus for the manufacture
Biological compost
Other organic fertilisers
from peat, brown coal, and similar vegetable deposits
Organic fertilisers containing added bacterial cultures, mycelia or the like
Fertilisers containing plant vitamins or hormones
Preparation of fertilisers characterised by biological or biochemical treatment steps, e.g. composting or fermentation
Treatments involving invertebrates, e.g. worms, flies or maggots
Addition or removal of substances other than water or air to or from the material during the treatment (for activating or stimulating the treatment C05F17/20)
using specific microorganisms or substances, e.g. enzymes, for activating or stimulating the treatment (use of invertebrates C05F17/05)
using irradiation, e.g. solar or nuclear radiation; using electric or magnetic fields
Treatment of liquids or slurries
Treatments combining two or more different biological or biochemical treatments, e.g. anaerobic and aerobic treatment or vermicomposting and aerobic treatment
Heating or cooling during the treatment
Controlling the treatment in response to process parameters
Separation, elimination or disposal of harmful substances during the treatment
Apparatus therefor
